threatModel: An ordinary remote peer controls HTTP method, request-target, authority, forwarding, and header bytes. Deliberately hostile same-process code and pre-bootstrap host compromise are excluded by SPEC section 6.6.

surfaces: direct Node HTTP/1 and HTTP/2 request conversion
generated Node and Vercel request conversion
raw method, request-target, Host/:authority, :scheme, and trusted-proxy reconstruction

promptFamily: Trace each scoped remote carrier from its raw Node representation through the Web Request boundary. Report any case where parsing or normalization changes security-relevant identity before rejection.

Compare direct HTTP/1, direct HTTP/2, generated Node, and generated Vercel behavior for method, target, authority, and scheme. Report any adapter differential that changes dispatch, authentication, CSRF, cache, or mutation behavior.

For each accepted carrier, identify the sole trusted source, grammar, snapshot point, and fail-closed verdict. Report missing hostile-value proof or a path that consults attacker-controlled fallback authority.

budget.tokenBudgetNote: The runtime exposes no enforceable per-audit token meter; comparability is bounded by one investigator, one turn, 30 wall-clock minutes, and the closed file/surface list.

seededCanaries.controlledFixture: An HTTP/2 peer sends :method=post; an unsafe adapter passes it directly to new Request(...), which exposes POST and dispatches a state-changing mutation.
seededCanaries.expectedRootCause: The raw case-sensitive HTTP method identity changed at the Node-to-Fetch bridge before policy and dispatch.

seededCanaries.controlledFixture: An HTTP/1 peer sends two Host fields; an unsafe adapter trusts Node's normalized headers.host value and constructs the request URL without checking rawHeaders.
seededCanaries.expectedRootCause: Normalization erased an ambiguous authority before the adapter enforced exactly one Host field.

deduplication.rule: Count one R root cause only when a remotely triggerable invariant failure is reproduced or proven at the audited SHA and no existing matrix/ledger row has the same violated invariant and enforcement door. Variants of an existing root cause are deduplicated to that row; an unproved suspicion is recorded as a follow-up, not as R.
